raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.0, "R1.2": 0.3, "R1.3": 0.0, "R1.4": 0.0, "R1.5": 0.3, "R1.6": 0.7, "R1.7": 0.0, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.6": "\"Improve this draft\" most directly signals sentence- and paragraph-level writing quality work — clarity, concision, flow, and technical register — making writing polish the central expected responsibility; however the delegation does not explicitly exclude structural or logical revision, so the weight is held at 0.7 pending clarification rather than raised to 1.0", "R1.2": "Logical consistency is peripherally relevant because improving a technical draft may incidentally expose premise-conclusion gaps (e.g. the unsupported claim that any LP-feasible schedule automatically satisfies the commitment constraint), but the delegation does not explicitly request logical review", "R1.5": "Structural reorganization is peripherally in scope because the paragraphs show ordering tensions (Combined strategy is introduced before the conservative LP is fully characterized), but the delegation gives no explicit signal that reordering is desired"}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Should I limit improvements to sentence-level writing quality (prose clarity, flow, and concision), or do you also want structural reorganization of the paragraphs and/or identification of logical gaps in the argument?"}

rationale.R1.6: "Improve this draft" most directly signals sentence- and paragraph-level writing quality work — clarity, concision, flow, and technical register — making writing polish the central expected responsibility; however the delegation does not explicitly exclude structural or logical revision, so the weight is held at 0.7 pending clarification rather than raised to 1.0
rationale.R1.2: Logical consistency is peripherally relevant because improving a technical draft may incidentally expose premise-conclusion gaps (e.g. the unsupported claim that any LP-feasible schedule automatically satisfies the commitment constraint), but the delegation does not explicitly request logical review
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is peripherally in scope because the paragraphs show ordering tensions (Combined strategy is introduced before the conservative LP is fully characterized), but the delegation gives no explicit signal that reordering is desired

parsed.clarification_question: Should I limit improvements to sentence-level writing quality (prose clarity, flow, and concision), or do you also want structural reorganization of the paragraphs and/or identification of logical gaps in the argument?
